content: I'm so frustrated. spent 4 hours on this bug and I'm no closer to fixing it
I hear you. Four hours on one bug is exhausting. What are you stuck on?
the agent keeps responding 'received' instead of the actual subagent result. I've traced it through three layers and the payload is correct each step but the final reply is wrong

Subagent return-payload drop. That bug pattern shows up when the result is captured in one scope but composed into the reply from another scope where the variable went stale. Have you checked the message-composition site rather than the subagent-completion site?
no I've been looking at completion. let me check composition

Worth a look. The completion side often gets the most attention because that's where you'd expect the bug to be — but bugs that survive 'payload is correct here' all the way through usually live in the LAST place that touches the value before it's emitted.

OH. found it. the message_compose function was hardcoded to use a default 'received' string when the optional payload was empty. but the payload wasn't empty — it was the optional wrapper that was none, even though the inner value was set
Classic optional-wrapping bug. Fix it and document the trap — anyone else looking at this code in 6 months will hit the same thing.
fixing now. thank you. I was about to lose my mind
Glad you got it. 4 hours on a bug then 5 minutes from someone fresh — happens to everyone. Take a break, you earned it.
yeah I'll grab some water. so relieved
